title: Psychotria paradoxalis
taxonomy: Psychotria paradoxalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Psychotria, which comprises approximately 1,800 species of tropical plants. Psychotria paradoxalis is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Rubiaceae family, also known as the coffee or madder family, is one of the largest families of flowering plants. This species is part of the Rubiaceae family due to its characteristic flowers and inflorescences.

intro: Psychotria paradoxalis, also known as Cat's Claw, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which have been used to treat various conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and even cancer. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria paradoxalis include alkaloids, particularly oxindole alkaloids, as well as flavonoids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cat's Claw include capsules, teas, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
